etymology.description: Original: semantic 爪(爪) (zhǎo, "hands") + phonetic 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn") + semantic 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") -> meaning uncertain -> Later: ideogrammic compound 母(母) (mǔ, "mother") + 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") + 女(女) (nǚ, "woman")
etymology.interpretation: Initially, the character combined 爪(爪) (zhǎo, "hands"), 角(角) (jiǎo, "horn"), and 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") for a meaning that remains uncertain. The later pictographic representation of 母(母) (mǔ, "mother") plus 中(中) (zhōng, "middle") and 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") suggests the idea of familial relations, particularly involving women, though specifics are unclear.
etymology.simplification: 娄 is the simplified form of the traditional 婁 by reducing complexity and likely through the removal of some components for ease of writing, maintaining just 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") to preserve minimal recognition.

examples.chinese: 他刚建造了一座新的大厦(他剛建造了一座新的大廈)，名字叫做娄星大厦(名字叫做婁星大廈)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gāng jiànzàole yī zuò xīn de dàshà, míngzì jiàozuò lóuxīng dàshà.
examples.english: He just built a new building called Louxing Tower.

examples.chinese: 我们住在一个叫娄底的地方(我們住在一個叫婁底的地方)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhù zài yīgè jiào Lóudǐ de dìfāng.
examples.english: We live in a place called Loudi.

examples.chinese: 娄是一个罕见的姓氏(婁是一個罕見的姓氏)。
examples.pinyin: Lóu shì yī gè hǎnjiàn de xìngshì.
